The main field · Almora, Uttarakhand

The Farm

One naali.
No shortcuts.
No chemicals.

Dhir's Promise is one naali of Almora hillside — roughly 200 square metres of soil that has never seen a synthetic fertiliser or a pesticide. Not because of a certificate. Because of a choice.

When chemical farming began spreading through the region and the younger generation left for the cities, this farm went the other direction. It was built from scratch, with intention, on land that the mountains have been tending for centuries. The name says everything: Dhairya — patience — is the only method here.

Year-round, multiple harvests, a growing variety of crops. Small enough that every plant is known. Big enough to matter.

1 naali of chemical-free Almora land 0 synthetic inputs. Ever.

What's Growing

The land decides
the season.

What we grow changes with the season, the soil, and what the land asks for. These are current crops — message us on WhatsApp to ask what's available right now.

Mustard
Sarson · Cold-Pressed Oil

Bright yellow flowers in winter. The seeds pressed cold into an oil that carries the full sharpness of Pahadi mustard — nothing added, nothing removed.

Available Now
Seasonal Greens
Palak · Dhania · Methi & More

Cut fresh and dispatched the same day. Palak, dhania, methi — whatever is ready. No cold chain, no days in transit. From our soil to your kitchen, fast.

Seasonal
Root Vegetables
Aloo · Gajar · Pyaaz · Adrak · Lahsun

Potatoes, carrots, onions, ginger, garlic — grown slowly in hill soil. Denser, more flavourful than valley produce. The altitude does what no additive can.

Seasonal

Also growing: peas, beans, zucchini, lauki, cucumber, lettuce, English daisy, mulberry. Corn and grapes coming soon.

Ask what's available today

बीज कोश

Beej Kosh —
The Seed Treasury.

The most endangered things in Kumaon aren't wildlife — they're seeds. Heirloom Pahadi varieties that took centuries to adapt to this altitude are disappearing as chemical farming standardises what grows here.

Beej Kosh is our effort to preserve them. We are currently building the collection — the coming harvest will fill it with onion, dhania, mustard, carrot, pea, radish, and spinach seeds. Watch this space.

Each seed packet comes with growing notes specific to Uttarakhand's climate and altitude. We only share what we've grown ourselves.

Rosemary Service

We plant it.
We tend it.
You enjoy it.

Rosemary grows extraordinarily well in Almora's climate — the altitude, the dry winters, the mountain air. Most people don't know that. We do. And we've turned that knowledge into a service.

01

Consultation & planting

We assess your space — garden, terrace, or pot — and plant the right variety for your conditions.

02

Online consultation (coming soon)

Paid one-on-one guidance sessions — pruning schedules, feeding, troubleshooting. From us to you, wherever you are.

03

Harvest whenever you need

Fresh, fragrant, chemical-free rosemary. From your own space, on your own time.

Ask the Farm

What would you like
to know?

Ask about crops, availability, growing advice, or the farm itself.

Namaste. I'm the Dhir's Promise farm assistant — ask me anything about what we grow, how we grow it, our seeds, rosemary service, or how to order. Patience is what we do here.
What's available right now? Tell me about the Beej Kosh How do I order? What makes this farm different?

Farm Visits

Come and see
what patience
looks like.

Dhir's Promise is a working farm, not a tourist attraction. But if you want to understand where your food comes from — really understand it — you're welcome here.

What a visit includes

A walk through what's currently growing, a conversation about the methods, the seeds, and the choices behind the farm. No script. Just the real thing.

  • Tour of the growing beds and seed bank
  • Harvest anything that's ready, take it with you
  • See the Beej Kosh collection up close
  • Learn about chemical-free growing at altitude
Arrange a Visit
